---
title: "Examples"
---

This page provides an example of how to compute and visualize distinction centrality using the classic Medici family network from Padgett & Ansell (1993).

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(igraph)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
source("Functions/distinction.R")

# Load the Medici dataset
medici_edges <- read_csv("Data/MediciEdgeList.csv")
g <- graph_from_data_frame(medici_edges, directed = FALSE)

# Calculate distinction centrality
results <- distinction(g)

# Display the top 10 most "distinguished" families
results %>%
  arrange(desc(scd)) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

### Analysis
The results show the Medici family's high distinction score, confirming their unique position of balancing power (status) while managing social constraints compared to other contemporary families.
